content: Pinpoint key phrases or numbers within the confirmed relevant text segment to extract the exact data point required, avoiding distraction from surrounding information. This ensures precision in retrieval from a focused context.

title: Reinterpret Ambiguous Likelihood Questions
description: When a financial question uses ambiguous "likelihood" phrasing without explicit probabilities, explore alternative interpretations.
content: If the text provides qualitative assessments (e.g., "reasonably possible") or timeframes instead of precise probabilities, consider if the question implicitly seeks a relevant duration or numerical period. This strategy helps derive a concrete answer when a direct probabilistic one is unavailable.

title: Leverage Numerical Output Constraints
description: Use specific output format requirements to guide interpretation when direct answers are not immediately evident.
content: If a question demands a precise numerical output (e.g., an integer with no decimal places) and initial interpretations yield only qualitative statements, re-examine the text for any directly applicable numerical values that fit the output constraint, even if it requires a slight re-framing of the question's intent.

title: Prioritize Temporal Cues in Resolution Queries
description: For questions about the timing or status of resolutions, prioritize explicit temporal phrases found in the context.
content: When evaluating uncertain financial positions like tax resolutions, if the text repeatedly mentions specific timeframes (e.g., "within a certain number of months or years") as part of the company's expectation, these temporal cues can provide the intended numerical answer, especially in the absence of definitive probability percentages.

title: Prioritize Cumulative Periods for Unspecified Requests
description: When a financial metric is requested without a specific reporting period, choose the most comprehensive cumulative period available.
content: For questions without explicit timeframes, prefer year-to-date or nine-month figures over shorter interim periods like three-month figures. This approach ensures consistency with standard financial reporting practices which often default to the longest available cumulative period.

title: Reconcile Discrepancies in Component Sums
description: If the sum of related line items does not precisely equal a clearly stated total, pause and thoroughly re-evaluate the definitions and interrelationships of all fields.
content: Do not prematurely conclude an inconsistency; instead, investigate if certain line items are already implicitly factored into a higher-level subtotal or if other unstated adjustments contribute to the final sum. This avoids hesitation when an item is correctly identified but appears to contradict a sum check.

title: Understand Complex Financial Statement Hierarchies
description: Recognize that the presentation of totals and sub-components in financial statements can be complex and not always a simple linear addition.
content: Pay close attention to the specific wording and indentation of line items and their corresponding totals. A 'net income attributable' might be structured such that listed 'continuing' and 'discontinued' items have specific accounting nuances that affect their direct additivity to the final reported total.

title: Multi-Criteria Data Identification
description: Efficiently locate specific financial data by simultaneously applying multiple filter criteria from the question.
content: When a question specifies several attributes (e.g., maturity, cost type, security type, date), use all criteria concurrently to pinpoint the exact cell or entry in a relevant table. This ensures precision and avoids misinterpreting partial matches.

title: Strategic Table Search
description: Prioritize searching for tables whose titles and structure directly align with the key financial concepts in the question.
content: If a question concerns a specific financial metric (e.g., amortized cost) broken down by a particular dimension (e.g., maturity), look for tables explicitly titled or structured to present that exact breakdown. This reduces search space and increases the likelihood of finding direct answers.

title: Direct Data Extraction
description: When the desired financial value is directly available in the identified source, extract it without performing additional computations.
content: After locating the precise data point, confirm if it directly answers the question. If so, record the value as-is, paying attention to any implicit or explicit formatting or precision requirements specified in the question.

title: Prioritize Direct Per-Share Values
description: Directly extract per-share values when explicitly requested, avoiding unnecessary calculations.
content: When a question asks for a per-share amount, first identify if the table contains a column that directly provides this per-share metric, such as "Weighted Average Fair Value." This often provides the answer directly without needing to calculate total amounts and then divide.

title: Locate Specific Activity Rows
description: Precisely identify the row corresponding to the financial activity mentioned in the question within summary tables.
content: In tables summarizing financial activities (e.g., share awards), ensure you navigate to the exact row that matches the specific event requested (e.g., "Forfeited," "Granted," "Vested"). This ensures accuracy in data extraction for the relevant transaction type.

title: Understand Weighted Average Fair Value
description: Recognize "Weighted Average Fair Value" as a per-share metric for specific financial events.
content: When reviewing financial tables detailing share activity, interpret "Weighted Average Grant-date Fair Value" as the average dollar value per share associated with that particular action. This figure often represents the direct per-share cost or value at the time of the event.

title: Understand the full scope of an event's financial impact
description: Recognize that an event like a settlement agreement can have indirect but causally linked financial consequences.
content: When a question asks for a benefit "from" a specific agreement, carefully identify all direct and indirect outcomes explicitly stated as resulting from that agreement, even if the benefit isn't explicitly phrased as "X from Y". Do not narrowly limit the search to only direct statements.

title: Avoid assuming zero for indirectly linked values
description: Do not default to a zero value when a specific financial impact isn't directly stated but is clearly quantified elsewhere and causally linked within the context.
content: Carefully evaluate if an unstated direct value implies zero, especially when a related, quantified value is present due to an indirect causal chain. A non-zero value linked through a conditional statement should be preferred over an assumed zero.

title: Trace causal connections across sentences
description: Thoroughly read and connect sentences describing multi-stage transactions or agreements to identify the full sequence of events and their financial implications.
content: Pay close attention to linking phrases (e.g., "as part of," "in return for," "consequently") that establish a causal relationship between an agreement or action and a subsequent regulatory approval or financial outcome. Missing such connections can lead to misattributing or overlooking relevant financial data.

title: 明确输出已识别的最终答案
description: 即使推理过程正确识别了答案，也必须确保将最终数值明确地输出。
content: 当推理步骤已成功确定或计算出目标数值后，务必采取最终行动，将其呈现在指定的答案字段中。正确的推理过程若缺乏明确的最终输出，将导致整体任务失败。

title: 区分初始预测与后续修订
description: 识别并提取问题中明确要求的初始预测值，而非后续批准或修订的数值。
content: 财务文件中常包含初始请求或预测，随后伴随各种调整、批准或重新考虑。当问题询问特定初始文件或时期的“预测”金额时，应确保仅提取该原始预测，并忽略后续修改，除非问题明确要求最终批准或修订值。

title: 确认请求的时间范围
description: 仔细核对问题中提及的特定年份或时期，确保提取的数据点与之精确匹配。
content: 仔细审视问题的措辞，以确定所要求财务指标的确切时间范围。例如，“2021年预测”指的是在特定年份进行的预测，这与在后期发生的预测或批准（即使是针对类似的成本项目）有所区别。

title: Contextual Data Filtering
description: Prioritize data extraction by first identifying the specific acquisition or event mentioned in the question.
content: Isolate the section of the financial document that pertains directly to the named transaction. This prevents confusion with other acquisitions or unrelated financial disclosures presented in the same document.

title: Direct Line Item Value Retrieval
description: When a question seeks a specific value, directly locate and extract it from the relevant financial table.
content: If the requested item is clearly listed as a line item in a table with its corresponding amount, simply read that value. Resist the urge to perform complex calculations if the answer is explicitly provided.

title: Leveraging Tabular Organization for Intangibles
description: Efficiently navigate purchase price allocation tables to find specific intangible asset values.
content: Recognize that purchase price allocation tables categorize acquired assets and liabilities. Locate the row corresponding to the specific intangible asset requested and retrieve its associated value from the amount column.

title: Prioritize Direct Statements
description: Locate and extract numerical values explicitly stated in the text when they directly address the question.
content: When a question seeks a specific financial figure, first scan the provided context for a phrase that precisely matches the requested information, including the specific date if applicable. If an exact match with the numerical value is found, extract it directly without further computation.

title: Align Question Keywords
description: Utilize key terms from the question to efficiently pinpoint relevant sections within the financial disclosure.
content: Break down the question into essential keywords and phrases (e.g., "expected future pre-tax compensation expense," "non-vested restricted shares"). Use these keywords to quickly navigate the document and identify the specific sentence or paragraph containing the answer.

title: Validate Calculation Necessity
description: Determine if a calculation is truly required or if the answer is already presented as a final figure.
content: After identifying relevant text, assess if the numerical value provided directly answers the question or if it's merely a component requiring further computation. Proceed with calculations only if the precise value requested is not explicitly stated.

title: Prioritize Direct Textual Information
description: Efficiently extract information by locating explicit statements in the text that directly answer the question.
content: When a question asks for a specific numerical value, first search for its direct mention within the provided context. This approach minimizes interpretation and calculation if the answer is already explicitly stated.

title: Target Information with Keyword Matching
description: Employ keywords from the question to quickly pinpoint relevant sections or sentences within lengthy financial documents.
content: Break down the question into its key terms (e.g., "Net periodic pension benefit") and use these to guide your search. This strategy significantly reduces reading time and improves accuracy in locating specific data points.

title: Validate Answer Format and Precision
description: Always confirm that the extracted or calculated answer adheres to any specified formatting requirements or precision levels.
content: Before finalizing an answer, cross-reference it with the question's stated requirements, such as the number of decimal places or units. This ensures the output is compliant and correctly formatted.

title: 确认关键数据来源
description: 在财务提取过程中，务必确认所有关键数值信息都明确存在于提供的上下文材料中，以避免引用不存在的数据。
content: 尽管轨迹最终识别出了正确的值，但它引用了一段在给定上下文中并未出现的关键信息。为防止对外部或虚构信息的依赖，未来应确保所有重要数据点都可直接追溯到输入文本。

title: 明确处理信息缺失
description: 当回答问题所需的财务数据未在提供的上下文中明确给出时，模型应明确指出信息缺失，而不是尝试推断或臆造。
content: 本问题所需的长期债务义务信息在提供的表格或描述性文本中缺失。在这种基本数据缺失的情况下，正确的做法是声明无法根据现有输入回答，以防止产生不准确或虚构的回复。

title: 警惕不完整的表格或章节
description: 当标题表明包含某类信息（如“资产和负债”），但实际内容却仅呈现部分（如只列出资产）时，应警惕关键数据可能在该特定部分中缺失。
content: 提供的上下文包含一个题为“终止经营的资产和负债摘要”的章节，但仅列出了资产部分。这种不完整性应促使代理检查负债数据可能在哪里，或指出其缺失，以避免提取遗漏。

title: Prioritize Direct Numerical Definitions
description: When a question asks for a specific numerical value, search for sentences that explicitly define or state that value for the queried concept.
content: Locate sentences that contain the target concept and directly assign a numerical value (e.g., percentage, amount) to it. Extract the stated number, ensuring it precisely answers the numerical aspect of the question.

title: Match Specific Plan or Program Names
description: Successfully identify and locate information pertaining to distinct plans, policies, or programs by matching their full names or acronyms.
content: Use the exact name of the plan (e.g., "Employee Stock Purchase Plan") provided in the question to navigate the document. This ensures that the extracted information is specific to the requested program.

title: Isolate the Core Numerical Request
description: Disregard supplementary details that describe conditions for determining a base value when the question specifically asks for a fixed percentage or ratio applied to that base.
content: When a percentage is sought, focus on the multiplier itself, not the method of calculating the underlying value it's applied to. For example, if a price is "X% of value A or value B, whichever is lower," and the question asks for the percentage, X is the answer, not "A or B, whichever is lower."

title: 区分实体所有权
description: 仔细核对问题所问的主体与文本中提及的所有权信息是否匹配。
content: 当问题询问公司整体的“流通股所有权百分比”时，需确认上下文是否提供了公司层面的流通股数据和所有权信息。若上下文仅提供子公司或资产的权益百分比，则应警惕实体不匹配，避免直接使用子公司数据作为主公司的答案。

title: 区分时间点所有权
description: 识别上下文中不同时间点（如收购前、收购后）或状态（如初始、最终）对应的所有权百分比，并选择与问题最匹配的。
content: 如果文本提供了资产或子公司在不同时间点的所有权比例（例如“之前持有X%，随后增加到Y%”），需要根据问题的具体意图选择正确的数值。避免在没有明确指示的情况下，默认选择最新或最大的数值。

title: 避免不当推断
description: 当明确判断上下文缺乏回答问题所需的核心数据时，避免强行从不完全匹配的信息中推断答案。
content: 如果问题要求的数据类型（如“流通股所有权”）在文本中完全缺失，即使有其他相关的百分比数据，也应首先报告数据不足。不应在缺乏直接证据或明确指引的情况下，从不相关或间接信息中推断出数值答案。

title: Prioritize Direct Statements
description: Locate and prioritize explicit statements within the text that directly answer the question's core subject.
content: Focus on sentences or phrases that contain the exact information requested, such as specific quantities or rights. This ensures accuracy by relying on unambiguous declarations rather than inferred or calculated values.

title: Filter Extraneous Details
description: Disregard surrounding information that is not directly pertinent to the specific question asked.
content: Efficiently navigate through financial documents by identifying and ignoring sections that discuss unrelated topics, even if they are in close proximity to the relevant data. This prevents cognitive overload and maintains focus on the critical details.

title: Validate Output Format
description: Always confirm that the final numerical answer adheres to any specified formatting or precision requirements.
content: Before concluding, review the question's instructions for numerical output, such as decimal places or units. Adjust the extracted value as necessary to meet these specific presentation criteria.

title: Distinguish Initial vs. Remaining Authorized Amounts
description: Clearly differentiate between the total initial authorized amount and the remaining authorized amount when both are present in the text.
content: A financial question asking for the "authorized amount" may refer to the total amount initially approved, not necessarily the portion that remains unused. Always consider the precise phrasing and context to avoid confusing these two distinct figures.

title: Reconcile Temporal Scopes of Data
description: Pay close attention to the time periods associated with different financial figures to avoid misinterpreting their implications.
content: Information about activities "during a specific period" does not negate or override figures reported "as of a specific date" that reflect cumulative activity. Ensure that the temporal scope of each data point is correctly understood and reconciled.

title: Clarify Ambiguous Financial Terms
description: When a financial term can refer to multiple related values, prioritize understanding the specific intent of the question.
content: If the context provides both an initial total authorization and a current remaining authorization, and the question is broadly phrased, consider which figure represents the overarching program authorization.

title: Clarify Semantic Meaning of Financial Terms
description: The model misidentified "Debt term" as the latest maturity year instead of a specific duration or a referenced term value.
content: Always consider all possible interpretations of ambiguous financial terms like "term" or "duration." Actively search for specific numerical values (e.g., months, years) directly representing the term rather than inferring it solely from maturity dates.

title: Scan for All Relevant Numerical Formats
description: The model focused exclusively on extracting years for the "Debt term," overlooking other numerical formats that could represent the term, such as durations in months or other specific values.
content: When extracting numerical answers, ensure the search strategy isn't limited to a specific format (e.g., years only). Broaden the search to include any numbers that could semantically fit the queried term, irrespective of their appearance as dates or explicit durations.

title: Confirm Outstanding Debt Status
description: The model assumed debt mentioned in change-of-control provisions was outstanding without explicit confirmation of its issuance or current status.
content: When identifying debt instruments for calculations or specific queries, prioritize information explicitly stating issuance or current outstanding status. Avoid assumptions about the active status of debt based solely on its mention in general clauses.

title: Correct Interpretation of Scaled Table Values
description: When a table's header specifies "in thousands" or similar, understand that the *displayed numerical values* within that column are already expressed in those scaled units, not that they are raw numbers requiring multiplication to reach thou
content: If a table indicates "Shares (in thousands)" and displays a value of "90," this signifies "90 thousand shares." The number "90" is already the quantity in thousands, and the error stemmed from interpreting "90" as 90 individual units needing to be scaled up by 1,000.

title: Prioritize Table's Presented Unit Over Implicit Conversion
description: If a question asks for a numeric value without specifying the unit (e.g., "Number of Shares"), but the source table presents that data in a scaled unit (e.g., "in thousands"), the answer should typically be given in the table's presented un
content: The trajectory erroneously converted "90" (from a "Number of Shares" column specified "in thousands") to "90,000" because the question did not explicitly state "in thousands." Absent explicit instruction to convert, assume the question implicitly refers to the unit provided by the source table.

title: Establish Clear Rules for Unit Ambiguity
description: Develop and adhere to clear internal rules for handling unit ambiguities, especially when table headers indicate scaling (e.g., "in thousands") but the question does not explicitly confirm the required output unit.
content: The trajectory exhibited extensive internal debate and contradictory interpretations regarding unit conversion, indicating a lack of a firm decision-making framework. Establish a consistent approach, such as "default to table's presented unit unless explicit conversion is requested," to prevent repetitive re-evaluation and errors.

title: Prioritize Direct Field Match for "Other" Qualifiers
description: When a question contains "Other" and a context table has a specific line item like "Other [Category]", prioritize selecting that direct line item.
content: Avoid broad interpretations of "Other" by trying to sum various separate items that could hypothetically be "other." Instead, look for the most direct and specific "Other" category provided in the data.

title: Assume Aggregated Value for "Current and Long-term" when Breakdown is Missing
description: If a question asks for a specific noncurrent component (e.g., "Noncurrent Liabilities, Other") and the data provides an aggregated "Current and Long-term" line item without further breakdown, assume the entire aggregated value is the intend
content: Do not attempt to arbitrarily split aggregated "current and long-term" figures if no explicit breakdown is given in the context. In such cases, the entire combined amount from the relevant "Other" line item should be considered.

title: Avoid Combining Distinct "Other" Categories
description: Do not sum multiple separate line items (e.g., "Net deferred taxes" and "Other current and long-term liabilities") when the question refers to "Other" and a specific, singular "Other" line item exists.
content: Recognize that "Other" in a question often refers to a singular, residual category explicitly labeled as such in financial tables. Do not incorrectly group disparate items into a calculated "Other" total when a direct match is present.

title: Prioritize Direct Information Extraction
description: Directly locate and extract explicitly stated values when keywords from the question match table row or column headers.
content: When a question asks for a specific metric (e.g., "Net change (shares)"), first look for a direct label match in tables or structured data. This often involves finding the exact row and column intersection.

title: Utilize Table Structure for Financial Data
description: Leverage the clear organization of financial tables, mapping question keywords to specific rows and columns to pinpoint the required data.
content: Financial documents frequently present data in organized tables. Efficiently navigate these tables by matching the requested metric with row labels and the desired unit (e.g., "shares") with column headers to retrieve the precise value.

title: Focus on Specific Data Sections
description: Identify and narrow down to the most relevant financial section indicated by the question's keywords.
content: Use keywords in the question (e.g., "treasury stock activity," "net change") to quickly identify the specific section or note in the financial statements that contains the answer, ignoring irrelevant data.

title: 谨慎解读叙述文本中的量纲词
description: 当叙述文本中包含明确的量纲词（例如“12 million shares”）时，产出的数值可能被预期为原始数字部分（如“12”），而非完全展开的数字（如“12,000,000”）。
content: 不要仅仅因为问题指定了基础单位就自动将诸如“X million”或“X thousand”这样的数字转换为其完整的数值形式。在财务语境中，通常会直接报告缩放后的数字（例如“X”），这暗示着输出单位本身就是缩放后的（例如以百万计）。

title: 参照周围表格或章节标题推断隐含单位缩放
description: 即使没有明确的文档级单位声明，邻近表格或章节标题中数字的缩放约定也能指示叙述文本值预期的输出规模。
content: 检查附近的表格、脚注或章节标题，查找诸如“(millions)”或“(thousands)”之类的短语。如果存在，这通常意味着所有相关的数值答案，包括从叙述文本中提取的数字，都应遵循相同的缩放约定，从而覆盖问题中对基础单位的字面解释。

title: 确认数值答案的预期精度和量纲
description: 理解给定财务信息提取任务中数值答案预期的典型精度和量纲（例如，整数、一位小数、以百万计、以千计）。
content: 财务信息提取任务通常对数值输出的精度和量纲有隐含的规则。应查阅示例参考答案或任务指南，以确定数字是应报告为绝对形式还是缩放形式（例如，12 与 12,000,000），以及是否应截断或包含小数位，即使问题指定了基础单位。

title: Locate Specific Data in Tables
description: Efficiently find requested financial figures by direct matching of question terms to table entries.
content: When a question asks for a specific financial item, immediately look for an exact or near-exact match of that item's name within the rows or descriptions of provided financial tables. This direct lookup is often the fastest path to the answer.

title: Cross-Reference Dates and Columns
description: Always ensure the extracted value corresponds to the correct reporting period.
content: After identifying the relevant row for a financial item, verify that the value taken is from the column header that matches the specific date or period requested in the question. Mistaking reporting periods is a common error to avoid.

title: Adhere to Output Formatting Guidelines
description: Present numerical answers according to specified precision and unit requirements.
content: Before finalizing an answer, review any instructions regarding decimal places or implicit units (e.g., "in millions"). If the source data provides whole numbers and no fractional part is specified, present the final answer as an integer.

title: Prioritize Direct Statement Extraction
description: When a specific metric is requested, first look for its explicit mention in narrative text or summary sentences.
content: Many financial data points, especially aggregate or weighted averages, are directly stated in descriptive paragraphs accompanying tables. Prioritize these direct statements as they often provide the precise answer without requiring calculation or interpretation from raw table data.

title: Precise Financial Terminology Matching
description: Carefully distinguish between similar financial terms or conditions to ensure the exact requested data point is identified.
content: Financial reports often present multiple, related figures (e.g., "options outstanding" versus "options exercisable"). Always match the terminology in the question precisely to the corresponding term in the document to select the correct value.

title: Utilize Narrative for Summary Metrics
description: Recognize that key summary statistics and aggregate values are often presented in the text surrounding detailed tables, rather than exclusively within the table rows.
content: Beyond structured tables, scan the introductory and concluding sentences of a section for summary data such as weighted averages, intrinsic values, or contractual lives. These narrative sections frequently consolidate information pertinent to the query.

title: Targeted Note Identification
description: Efficiently locate the specific financial statement note most likely to contain the requested information.
content: When a question pertains to a specific financial item, such as "Taxes on Earnings," prioritize searching for the corresponding dedicated note within the financial statements. This approach quickly narrows down the search scope, avoiding irrelevant sections.

title: Direct Information Extraction
description: Extract information directly stated in the text when it explicitly answers the question.
content: Once the relevant section is identified, look for sentences that explicitly provide the numerical answer without requiring complex calculations or interpretations. Trust direct statements as the primary source for the requested data.

title: Keyword-Driven Search
description: Employ specific keywords from the question to pinpoint precise information within a document.
content: Scan the identified relevant financial note for keywords directly from the question, such as "tax expense," "prior year," or "resolution." This strategy helps quickly locate the exact sentence or phrase containing the required information.

title: Unspecified Year - Prioritize Non-Zero Values
description: When a query asks for a line item without specifying a year, do not automatically default to a zero or missing value if non-zero values exist in prior periods.
content: Always review all available years to identify the most relevant data. If the most recent period is zero or blank for the requested item, consider selecting the last non-zero value from earlier periods, especially for adjustments or infrequent items.

title: Extract Absolute Values for Signed Financial Data
description: For financial items presented with negative signs or in parentheses to indicate losses or reductions, consider whether the question expects the absolute magnitude of the value.
content: If the extracted value is negative and other context suggests a positive value, extract the absolute value. This is common for adjustments, hedges, or items that can fluctuate between gain and loss.

title: Strategic Section Identification
description: Prioritize using document sections and headers to quickly narrow down information.
content: Efficiently locate relevant data by focusing on section titles and contextual cues provided in the document's structure. This approach helps in quickly navigating large financial documents to the most pertinent information.

title: Precise Table Data Extraction
description: Accurately extract information from tables by matching row and column headers.
content: When extracting numerical data from tables, carefully align the specific row criteria (e.g., a particular period) with the required column criteria (e.g., a specific category of commitment) to ensure precision. This minimizes errors in data retrieval.

title: Contextual Verification and Formatting
description: Always verify the extracted value against the question's specific requirements and apply necessary formatting.
content: Before finalizing an answer, confirm that the extracted value directly addresses all aspects of the question, including any explicit or implicit requirements for numerical precision or units, and perform any necessary adjustments.

title: Precise Keyword Matching
description: Focus on directly locating text segments that precisely address the question's core terms and conditions.
content: When seeking specific financial impacts, use keywords from the question to pinpoint the exact explanatory phrase or sentence in the provided text. Prioritize direct statements over inferring or combining information if an explicit answer is present.

title: Isolate Causal Factors
description: Verify that the identified financial impact is solely attributable to the specified causes, distinguishing it from other concurrent adjustments.
content: After identifying a potential answer, cross-reference the context to ensure the reported change is *only* due to the factors mentioned in the question. Be mindful of lists of adjustments and confirm that the chosen value isn't a sum or net effect of other distinct drivers.

title: Disaggregate Composite Adjustments
description: Effectively navigate lists of multiple adjustments to identify the specific component relevant to the query.
content: When a total change is broken down into several distinct sub-adjustments, carefully read each item to isolate the value corresponding to the exact criteria in the question. Avoid aggregating unrelated adjustments or overlooking the specific detail within a broader explanatory section.

title: Clarify "Loss Contingency" Scope
description: Ensure the definition of "loss contingency" aligns with the question's expected output, which might include specific monetary values related to potential gains or sums sought by the company.
content: When asked about "loss contingencies" and "damages sought," evaluate whether the scope includes monetary values involved in legal matters where the company is a claimant (seeking money), not just a defendant (facing claims). A broader interpretation might be necessary if specific amounts are mentioned.

title: Account for Company's Role as Claimant
description: Do not exclude specific monetary amounts associated with legal disputes solely because the company is the claimant seeking the funds, as the question may still expect this value.
content: When processing legal or arbitration details, clearly distinguish if the company is a claimant (seeking a specific sum) or a defendant (potentially liable for a sum). If a specific monetary value is sought by the company, consider its relevance to the question, even if it represents a potential gain rather than a loss for the company.

title: Retain Explicit Monetary Figures from Contingencies
description: Always prioritize the retention of explicitly stated monetary values associated with legal or contingent events found in the text.
content: If a specific dollar amount is clearly stated in connection with a legal or arbitration matter, ensure it is not prematurely discarded. First, identify all such concrete numerical values, then apply classification rules, being wary of overly strict interpretations that might lead to omitting the correct answer.

title: Prioritize Scheduled Obligations Data
description: Locate specific schedules that break down financial obligations into annual or periodic payments.
content: When asked for a payment in a specific future period, first search for tables detailing "scheduled payments" or "minimum obligations" by year. These tables provide the most granular and relevant information for future cash flows.

title: Map Relative Time to Absolute Periods
description: Systematically align relative time references (e.g., "Year Three") with the absolute periods presented in financial statements.
content: Establish a clear chronological mapping from the implicit or explicit starting point (e.g., the report date or the first listed period in a schedule) to the requested relative year.

title: Differentiate Total vs. Scheduled Liabilities
description: Distinguish between aggregate liability figures reported for a period and detailed periodic payment schedules for the same financial instrument.
content: Understand that a balance sheet item showing a total obligation (e.g., total finance lease liability) is distinct from a schedule detailing individual payments due in future years. Use the specific payment schedule for questions about annual amounts.

title: Prevent Period Ambiguity in Unspecified Questions
description: Do not automatically assume the most recent period when a financial question lacks an explicit date.
content: Financial tables often display data for multiple periods, with the most recent typically presented first. When the question does not specify a year or reporting period, carefully ascertain the intended period, as relying solely on the most prominent column can lead to incorrect data extraction.

title: Account for Absolute Value Expectation in "Loss" Scenarios
description: When extracting a "net gain (loss)" amount, clarify if the signed value or its absolute magnitude is expected, especially for losses.
content: A reported loss is typically shown as a negative value or in parentheses. If the question asks for "net gain (loss)" and the gold answer is positive despite the report showing a negative value for a loss, it implies an expectation of the absolute value, necessitating careful interpretation of the financial term.

title: Prioritize Explicit Statements
description: Prioritize extracting numerical answers explicitly stated in the text that directly match the question's intent.
content: When a question asks for a specific financial metric, search for sentences that directly report that metric and its value, minimizing interpretation or calculation. This approach is highly effective for questions with direct factual answers.

title: Match Question Keywords to Textual Phrases
description: Map key terms from the question to corresponding phrases in the financial text to locate relevant data.
content: Look for exact or very close matches between the question's terms (e.g., "Gain on sale") and phrases used in the document (e.g., "resulting in a gain of"). This direct alignment often points to the precise data required.

title: Confirm Output Formatting Rules
description: Always confirm that the extracted answer conforms to any explicit formatting requirements, such as decimal places or units.
content: Before finalizing an answer, review the problem's output instructions regarding precision, decimal places, or units, even if no modification to the raw extracted value is necessary. This ensures full compliance with output specifications.

title: Verify Data Context
description: Ensure all numerical figures used in calculations are explicitly sourced from and present within the provided text context.
content: The trajectory introduced an $18 million figure as a civil settlement without it being present in the given context. Always confirm the explicit presence of financial data in the provided document to prevent the use of unverified or external information.

title: Interpret Singular Queries
description: When a question asks for a singular "amount," carefully evaluate whether multiple identified figures should be aggregated or if a specific, most relevant one is sought.
content: The question asked for "Civil settlement amount" (singular), but the trajectory aggregated two distinct civil settlement figures. For singular "amount" queries, consider if there are implicit criteria for selecting a primary value rather than automatically summing all relevant figures.

title: Distinguish Settlement Specifics
description: Recognize that "civil settlement" can be a broad term; evaluate if the question implies a more specific type of settlement based on its nature or authority.
content: The trajectory treated a class action settlement and a regulatory penalty as equivalent "civil settlements" for aggregation. Pay attention to the specific nature and parties involved in settlements, as the question might implicitly refer to a particular type, such as regulatory fines or specific litigation outcomes.

title: Direct Line Item Matching
description: Precisely locate the requested financial metric by matching keywords or exact phrases from the question to the statement's line items.
content: Scan the relevant section of the financial statement (e.g., Cash Flows from Financing Activities) for the exact wording of the queried item. This ensures accurate data extraction without confusion from similar entries.

title: Temporal Data Alignment
description: Always confirm the specific financial reporting period required by the question and extract data from the corresponding column.
content: After identifying the correct line item, ensure the value is selected from the column representing the specified date or period, such as a particular year or interim reporting period. Misaligning the period can lead to incorrect answers.

title: Output Formatting Adherence
description: Strictly adhere to all specified output formatting requirements for numerical answers, including decimal places and rounding.
content: Before outputting the final answer, review the problem's instructions for any specific formatting rules. Adjust the extracted numerical value (e.g., rounding to the nearest whole number) to match these requirements precisely.

title: Prioritize Explicit Cost Basis
description: When calculating aggregate cost, actively seek and extract only explicitly stated "original purchase cost" or "originally purchased for" figures.
content: Focus on identifying the initial investment amount. Do not assume or infer cost from other figures like fair value, carrying value, or earnings adjustments if the original cost is not explicitly provided.

title: Differentiate Cost from Market or Book Values
description: Clearly distinguish between an investment's initial cost and its current market value, carrying value, or recognized income/gains/losses.
content: Recognize that fair value adjustments, adjusted fair values, carrying values, dividend income, or equity earnings are distinct from the original investment cost. Only use the original cost when "aggregate cost" is requested.

title: Exclude Investments Without Explicit Cost Data
description: If an investment is mentioned but its original purchase cost is not explicitly stated, gracefully exclude it from the aggregate cost calculation.
content: Do not include an investment in the sum if the text provides no explicit original cost, even if other financial details about that investment are present. Acknowledge the data absence and proceed with available information.

title: 避免重复扫描信息
description: 反复阅读相同文本而未能找到答案，表明信息搜索策略可能效率低下。
content: 实施策略来检测信息提取过程中无效的重复循环。如果在多次迭代后仍未识别出新信息，请重新评估搜索方法或找出僵局的原因。

title: 区分具体阈值与一般时间线
description: 将一般逾期或核销政策与特定收款审查阈值混淆会导致答案错误。
content: 仔细区分问题中关于特定审查标准与更广泛运营定义（如“逾期”或“核销”）的措辞。确保提取的时长直接匹配所要求的审查触发条件。

title: 识别并声明上下文不足
description: 尽管经过仔细扫描仍持续无法找到特定数据，往往表明所需信息在提供文本中缺失。
content: 如果在仔细审阅后仍无法找到特定问题的信息，并且文本暗示了不同类别的不同规则，请明确指出相关数据可能在给定上下文中缺失。

title: Direct Information Retrieval
description: Prioritize direct extraction when the question explicitly asks for a specific value or expectation.
content: When a question seeks a distinct numerical figure or a forward-looking statement, scan the document for a single sentence that provides this information verbatim. This often negates the need for complex calculations or data synthesis.

title: Strategic Keyword Matching
description: Use keywords from the question to efficiently pinpoint relevant sections and sentences within financial narratives.
content: Deconstruct the question into its core components (e.g., "reduction," "unrecognized tax benefits," "next 12 months"). Scan the text for these specific terms or their close synonyms to quickly navigate to the pertinent disclosure.

title: Identifying Forward-Looking Language
description: Recognize phrases that signal management's expectations, beliefs, or reasonably possible future outcomes.
content: When a question asks for an expected or estimated future event, look for explicit phrases like "reasonably possible," "believe," or "expect." These indicators often directly precede the forecasted amount or event.

title: Prioritize Type of Value Over Monetary Assumption
description: Do not default to monetary values when a question specifies a non-monetary attribute like "term" or "count," even in financial contexts.
content: When the question asks for a specific attribute such as "term of contract," prioritize locating the explicit mention of that attribute and its corresponding value (e.g., duration in months or years), rather than assuming a financial amount. Avoid defaulting to monetary figures if a non-monetary numerical value is explicitly sought.

title: Validate Direct Textual Matches for Keywords
description: When an explicit value directly matches keywords in the question (e.g., "term of contract" leading to "12 months"), validate if this is the intended answer before searching for alternatives.
content: If the text provides a direct numerical answer for a specified attribute (e.g., "term" equals "12 months"), confirm its relevance to the question. Do not dismiss such direct matches in favor of other numeric data points, especially when the question's phrasing clearly indicates the type of value expected.

title: Interpret Ambiguous Numerical Requests Carefully
description: For questions that imply a number without specifying currency or unit (e.g., "term of contract"), consider all types of numerical values found, such as durations, counts, or percentages, alongside monetary values.
content: When a question asks for a "number" related to a descriptive term like "term of contract," carefully evaluate whether the requested number refers to a duration, quantity, or monetary value. Do not prematurely restrict the search to only financial figures, but consider all numerically expressed attributes that directly correspond to the question's phrasing.

title: Locate Explicit Financial Figures
description: Prioritize direct extraction of numerical values when a specific financial item is requested.
content: When a question seeks a precise monetary figure for a financial item, scan the text for explicit statements that directly provide the value. This often involves matching the financial item keyword with its stated amount.

title: Validate Temporal Alignment of Financial Data
description: Always confirm that extracted financial figures correspond to the specified reporting period.
content: Financial figures are often time-sensitive. Ensure that the numerical data retrieved explicitly relates to the date or period mentioned in the question, ignoring figures from other periods.

title: Keyword-Driven Information Retrieval
description: Use keywords from the question to efficiently pinpoint relevant sections within extensive financial text.
content: For questions asking about specific financial components, employ a targeted search for these key terms within the document. This helps to quickly narrow down the relevant passages for detailed reading and extraction.

title: Prioritize Direct Data Extraction
description: When a specific metric is requested, first look for its explicit mention in tables or text.
content: Efficiently scan the provided document for tables or sentences that directly present the value asked in the question. Avoid immediate calculations if the required data is explicitly stated.

title: Contextual Table Lookup
description: Systematically navigate tables using question keywords to isolate the correct data point.
content: Identify the specific entity or category mentioned in the question (e.g., "Finance leases") to locate the appropriate row, and then use the requested metric (e.g., "Discount Rate") to find the corresponding column within that row.

title: Confirm Output Precision and Format
description: Always cross-reference the extracted value with any specified output format or precision requirements.
content: Before finalizing an answer, ensure it adheres to all stated constraints, such as the number of decimal places, percentage representation, or units, as detailed in the question or instructions.

title: Clarify "Granted (in dollars per share)" Interpretation
description: Understand that "Granted (in dollars per share)" can refer to different metrics depending on the type of stock award.
content: When a question asks for "Granted (in dollars per share)", recognize that for stock options, this often refers to the exercise price, while for other awards like RSUs or PSUs, it might refer to the grant date fair value. Carefully identify the specific metric the question seeks for the relevant award type.

title: Delimit Scope for Ambiguous "Granted" Questions
description: If a question about "Granted (in dollars per share)" is broad, analyze the context to determine if it implicitly refers to a specific type of award.
content: When a financial question uses general terminology like "Granted (in dollars per share)" without specifying the award type, examine the provided data and any potential gold answers for clues. Avoid attempting to aggregate across dissimilar award types if the context suggests a more specific focus, as this can lead to unfeasible calculations.

title: Differentiate Stock Option Exercise Price and Grant Fair Value
description: Recognize that for stock options, the "weighted average exercise price" and "weighted average grant date fair value" are distinct values.
content: For stock options, it is crucial to differentiate between the "weighted average exercise price" (the price at which shares can be purchased) and the "weighted average grant date fair value" (the estimated accounting value of the option). Select the appropriate value based on what the question explicitly or implicitly asks for.

title: Focus on Direct Basis/Rates
description: When a question asks for a "number" and the context explicitly provides a "basis" or "rate" for that number, consider if the rate itself is the intended answer, especially when absolute values are not fully derivable.
content: The trajectory initially identified the "1.9 shares" as the basis for shares added back but then spent considerable time attempting to calculate an absolute number. If the text provides a clear ratio or basis for a quantity, and a specific absolute quantity cannot be calculated due to missing information, prioritize the directly stated basis.

title: Interpret Question Ambiguity Carefully
description: When a question's phrasing, such as "Number of shares added back," can be interpreted as either a specific quantity or a rate/basis, carefully evaluate what information is available and what type of answer is most directly supported.
content: The model struggled with the ambiguity of whether an absolute count or a ratio was required. Future attempts should prioritize the most direct and explicitly stated information when facing such ambiguity, rather than forcing complex derivations.

title: Avoid Forcing Calculations with Incomplete Data
description: Do not initiate or persist with complex calculations to derive an absolute quantity when critical input data for such a calculation is explicitly missing from the context.
content: The trajectory attempted to calculate an absolute number of shares added back using tax benefits and fair value per share, despite acknowledging that the exact number of shares withheld was not provided. If data for a complete calculation is missing, re-evaluate if a calculation is truly required.

title: Prioritize Explicit Financial Disclosures
description: Trust and prioritize explicit allocation amounts provided in financial statements for specific line items.
content: When a financial statement directly provides an allocated value for an item (e.g., noncontrolling interest) as part of a business combination, use this figure. This direct disclosure should take precedence over values derived through calculations from other parts of the acquisition details.

title: Interpret Accounting Signs in Allocations
description: Understand the accounting implications of positive or negative signs for allocated amounts in acquisition contexts.
content: A negative sign for an allocated item like "redeemable noncontrolling interests" indicates its financial treatment, such as being classified as a liability or a reduction in equity, rather than a negative fair value. The absolute value still represents the fair value amount.

title: Apply Relevant Accounting Standards
description: Connect specific financial events to their governing accounting standards to guide valuation and recognition.
content: Reference applicable accounting standards, such as ASC 805 for business combinations, to confirm the required treatment (e.g., recording noncontrolling interests at acquisition-date fair value). This ensures alignment with established principles.

title: Prioritize Direct Share Price References
description: Focus on explicit statements of share price from market-related transactions rather than derived or related financial metrics.
content: When asked for "share price," seek direct mentions of the price per share involved in actual stock transactions, such as repurchases or sales. Distinguish these from accounting-specific values like weighted average exercise prices or intrinsic values, which do not represent the market price of common stock.

title: Locate Relevant Share Transaction Sections
description: Identify sections describing share-related corporate actions, as they often contain explicit pricing details.
content: Scan financial documents for sections detailing share repurchases, issuances, or other significant stock-related agreements. These areas frequently provide the precise price per share at which transactions occurred, serving as direct indicators of market price.

title: Differentiate Market Price from Equity Compensation Metrics
description: Clearly separate the common stock's market trading price from prices associated with stock options, SARs, or other equity compensation calculations.
content: The "share price" refers to the current or recent market value of common stock. Weighted average exercise prices, remaining contractual terms, and aggregate intrinsic values are specific to stock option and SAR activity and are distinct from the market price of the company's shares.

title: 区分授权股份与可用股份
description: 防止将已授权的股份总数与当前可用于授予的股份混淆。
content: 在提取股份相关数据时，请仔细阅读描述，区分“已授权股份”（最大允许数量）和“可用于未来授予的股份”（当前未授予余额），确保所提取的值精确对应问题中要求的术语。

title: 准确应用单位比例因子
description: 避免将通用的比例因子（如“千”）错误地应用于已明确标示单位的特定财务数据。
content: 在应用如“以千计”的通用比例因子之前，请确认所提取的特定数值是否已在原文中明确标示其最终单位，例如“股”，以防止重复或错误的单位转换。

title: 遵守数字格式规范
description: 确保从源文本到最终答案的数字格式（包括分隔符）得到保留。
content: 提取数值时，应一丝不苟地复制其原始格式，例如千位分隔符的使用，除非问题或输出要求有明确指示，以避免因细微差异而被标记为不准确。

title: Precise Table Data Location
description: Efficiently locate specific financial data by directly matching question keywords to table row and column labels.
content: Systematically scan financial tables to identify the row header that explicitly matches a key phrase in the query. Then, locate the column header that represents the requested metric and extract the value at their intersection.

title: Adhere to Output Precision
description: Always review the required numerical precision and formatting for the final answer.
content: Before finalizing an answer, confirm if the question specifies a particular decimal place count, unit, or format. Adjust the extracted value as necessary to meet these exact output criteria.

title: Direct Line Item Mapping
description: When a query refers to a specific, clearly defined financial status or line item, prioritize direct table lookup.
content: Recognize that some questions are designed for direct extraction of a single, explicitly labeled financial data point. Focus on identifying the row or entry that perfectly matches the detailed description in the question, rather than performing calculations.

title: Clarify "Dollars Per Share" Interpretation
description: The question "Shares issued (in dollars per share)" can refer to various per-share values like par value, issue price, or net proceeds.
content: When "dollars per share" is requested for issued shares, carefully evaluate whether the question seeks the par value, the gross issue price, or the net proceeds per share received by the company. Distinguish these based on contextual keywords and typical financial terminology.

title: Prioritize General Share Characteristics
description: The model incorrectly focused on the net proceeds of a single, recent issuance rather than a broader, fundamental share characteristic.
content: For general questions about "shares issued (in dollars per share)," prioritize general attributes like par value, which often apply consistently to a class of shares, over specific transaction-level financial outcomes like net proceeds from a particular offering.

title: Identify Explicit Par Value
description: The trajectory overlooked the explicit mention of "par value per share" as a direct answer to the per-share value question.
content: Pay close attention to explicit statements of "par value per share" within the text. This fundamental accounting value is a strong candidate for "dollars per share" questions related to issued stock, especially when stated alongside other share details.

title: Direct Information Retrieval
description: Prioritize extracting information that directly and explicitly answers the question.
content: When the question asks for a specific factual data point, focus on finding sentences or phrases in the text that state the requested information unequivocally. Avoid inference or complex calculations if a direct answer is present.

title: Question-Context Keyword Alignment
description: Use keywords from the question to efficiently pinpoint relevant sections within the provided context.
content: Identify the core terms in the question and scan the document for these matching keywords. This strategy helps to quickly locate the most pertinent sentences or paragraphs that are likely to contain the answer.

title: Isolate Specific Entities
description: Extract values that specifically pertain to the exact entity or metric requested by the question.
content: Carefully differentiate between various categories or types of assets mentioned in the text. Ensure the chosen numerical value directly corresponds to the specific item (e.g., communities vs. homes, or different types of properties) that the question is asking about.

title: Interpret Question Acronyms
description: Always deconstruct acronyms within the question to ensure a precise understanding of the requested information.
content: For questions containing common financial or temporal acronyms (e.g., EOY for End-of-Year, YTD for Year-to-Date), explicitly translate them to guide the search for specific data points. This prevents misinterpreting the target period or metric.

title: Broaden Search Beyond Primary Tables
description: Systematically review all contextual information, including footnotes and narrative descriptions, not just the main data tables.
content: Financial documents often embed critical macroeconomic assumptions, forecasts, or qualitative details in explanatory text, footnotes, or appendices that directly relate to numbers presented in tables. These textual elements can be the sole source for certain types of information, such as economic projections.

title: Align Forecast Period with Question's Specificity
description: When encountering multiple forecast values or periods, precisely match the extracted data to the exact temporal requirement specified in the question.
content: Differentiate between peak forecasts, current period figures, and future period projections. Ensure the selected data point (e.g., end of the current year, next year, or a specific quarter) directly corresponds to the question's time frame.

title: Ambiguous Unit Qualifiers
description: Pay close attention to unit qualifiers in both table headers (e.g., "(in thousands)") and question prompts (e.g., "(in shares)") to determine the expected output scale.
content: When a table column explicitly states "Shares (in thousands)" and the question asks for "Exercised (in shares)", avoid automatic unit conversion without confirming the specific dataset's convention. The question's parenthetical might refer to the data's presentation unit rather than demanding an absolute single unit count.

title: Parentheses Interpretation
description: Correctly interpret numerical values enclosed in parentheses, understanding when they denote negative numbers versus simply indicating a quantity or presentation style.
content: In financial tables, parentheses around a number (e.g., "(5)") typically signify a negative value. However, in contexts like "shares exercised" or "units granted", they might simply be a formatting choice for positive quantities, or even represent an outflow without necessarily being mathematically negative. Always check the context and related accounting principles.

title: Direct Extraction Preference for Ambiguous Units
description: When a direct value in a table matches the requested item but has an associated scaling notation, prioritize extracting the raw table value if the question's unit specification is ambiguous.
content: If a table column header states "Shares (in thousands)" and the question asks for "Exercised (in shares)", and a direct numerical entry (e.g., 5) exists, consider extracting this number directly. Do not automatically convert to absolute shares (e.g., 5,000) if the question's parenthetical "in shares" could be interpreted as referring to the scaled unit presented in the table.

title: Validate Contextual Scope
description: The model initiated its search by attempting to extract data from a time period (e.g., 2021) that was not present in the provided table context.
content: Before initiating data extraction, always verify that the requested time scope (e.g., specific years or quarters) aligns with the temporal coverage of the provided tables. Limit searches strictly to the available context to prevent irrelevant or non-existent data lookups.

title: Precisely Identify Nested Fields
description: The model struggled to consistently identify the specific "Shares" sub-column located under the "Common Stock" main header.
content: When table headers are hierarchical, ensure a precise mapping to extract values from the correct sub-column. Clearly differentiate between related but distinct data fields, paying close attention to both parent and child headers.

title: Avoid Referencing External or Omitted Data
description: The model attempted to locate and use data (e.g., referring to '454 shares' or general 2021 data) that was not explicitly present in the provided table snippet.
content: Strictly adhere to the provided context and avoid inferring or retrieving information from external or unprovided data sources. Only process data that is explicitly available within the given tables to prevent errors from data omission.

title: Targeted Information Retrieval
description: Prioritize understanding the specific information required by the question before scanning the text.
content: Formulate a precise search query based on the question's keywords. This targeted approach helps quickly locate the exact data points, reducing the processing of irrelevant information.

title: Direct Value Extraction
description: When keywords are directly matched, extract the explicitly stated value associated with them.
content: Once the relevant sentence or phrase containing the question's keywords is located, directly retrieve the explicit value presented alongside it. Avoid inferring or calculating if a direct statement exists.

title: Output Precision Verification
description: Always confirm that the extracted data conforms to any specified output format or precision requirements.
content: Before finalizing an answer, verify that the extracted value adheres to specified units, decimal places, or other formatting rules. This ensures the answer is presented correctly and precisely.

title: Understand related financial terminology
description: Identify and correctly interpret closely related financial terms like "facility fee" and "commitment fee" to ensure all relevant information is considered.
content: The model overlooked the significance of "facility fee" adjustments because it did not link it effectively to the "commitment fee" mentioned in the question, thus missing relevant contextual data.

title: Interpret directly stated percentage changes
description: Accurately interpret explicit percentage figures mentioned in the text as direct answers to questions about increases or decreases.
content: The model misread "adjustments can be up to 0.01% per annum on the facility fee," failing to recognize this directly as the answer for the "Increase (Decrease) In Percentage Commitment Fee."

title: Broaden interpretation of "increase (decrease)" questions
description: Do not limit the interpretation of "increase (decrease)" questions solely to requiring a calculation between two distinct values.
content: The model prematurely concluded no calculation could be performed due to the absence of explicit baseline and prior percentages, overlooking that a direct statement of a change or maximum adjustment can fulfill the question's requirement.

title: Locate Specific Financial Line Items
description: Accurately identify the exact financial metric requested by directly matching the query to a line item in the provided statement.
content: Always begin by scanning the financial document for the precise wording of the question to ensure the correct row is selected. This direct matching prevents misinterpretation and focuses the subsequent data extraction.

title: Select the Correct Reporting Period
description: After locating the line item, identify and extract the value corresponding to the specified or most relevant reporting period.
content: Pay close attention to column headers, which typically indicate different reporting periods. Ensure the extracted value aligns with the period of interest to prevent using outdated or incorrect data.

title: Confirm Units and Formatting Requirements
description: Verify the stated units of measure (e.g., millions, thousands) and adhere to any specified decimal place or formatting requirements for the final answer.
content: Always check the parenthetical information or footnotes for the unit of measure applicable to the entire table. Adjust the extracted numerical value as needed to match the required format for the final output.

title: 验证显式行项目
description: 确保请求的财务指标标签在提供的表格中明确存在。
content: 在尝试提取任何值之前，请确认所请求的行项目文本在财务报表中明确出现。不要基于一般概念假设或推断行项目的存在。

title: 避免数据幻觉
description: 不要为在提供的财务上下文中未明确列出的行项目或概念创建或假设值。
content: 如果特定财务指标或其相关值未在表格中明确显示，请勿凭空捏造。报告数据的缺失，而不是编造条目。

title: 未指定年份数据提取策略
description: 当未要求特定年份时，如果仅某个年份为项目提供了相关非零金额，应优先选择明确的数值而非占位符。
content: 如果问题未指定年份且提供了多个年份的数据，请寻找最相关的明确数值。破折号通常表示零，但如果其他年份有特定的非零交易，这个特定值可能是未指定查询的预期答案。

title: Direct Fact Retrieval
description: Directly extract information by matching key terms in the question to phrases in the text.
content: When a query asks for a specific financial item, locate the exact phrasing in the context that defines or quantifies that item. This approach prioritizes precision and direct identification of the requested data point.

title: Numerical Value Extraction
description: Identify and extract specific numerical values associated with financial metrics or conditions.
content: Scan the text for numbers directly preceding or following the identified key phrases related to the query. Ensure the extracted number is the precise quantity linked to the financial attribute in question.

title: Implied Unit Interpretation
description: Understand and appropriately handle implied units or scale (e.g., billions, millions) when extracting numerical answers.
content: Extract the numerical component as given, recognizing that large scale units like "billion" are often implied by the context and may not need to be explicitly included in the final numerical answer, but their presence in the text confirms the correct magnitude.

title: Prioritize Direct Statement Extraction
description: Efficiently locate and extract explicitly stated values when a question directly asks for a specific metric.
content: Scan the document for exact keywords from the question. If a precise numerical value is provided directly in conjunction with the queried term, extract it as the answer, avoiding unnecessary calculations.

title: Confirm Granularity and Scope
description: Verify that the extracted numerical value represents the full scope (e.g., total, entire period) requested by the question.
content: After identifying a potential answer, ensure it aligns with the question's implied or explicit timeframe or aggregation level. Differentiate between total amounts and period-specific components to avoid partial answers.

title: Validate Units and Precision
description: Confirm that the extracted numerical value's units and decimal precision match the expected format for the final answer.
content: Review the magnitude (e.g., millions, thousands) and decimal places of the found number. This step prevents common errors related to scaling or rounding, ensuring the answer is presented correctly.

title: Prioritize Direct Textual Extraction
description: Focus on identifying exact phrases or keywords from the question to locate the most direct answer within the provided text.
content: When a question seeks a specific data point, scan the context for the precise terminology used in the question. This strategy allows for direct extraction of the answer without requiring further interpretation or calculation when the information is explicitly stated.

title: Filter Context for Specificity
description: Distinguish between general background information and the precise data needed to answer a highly specific question.
content: Not all provided context is relevant to every question. Efficiently identify the core subject of the question and filter out extensive details or sub-sections that do not directly pertain to finding the requested data point, ignoring extraneous information that could distract.

title: Validate Answer Against Output Requirements
description: Before finalizing, cross-reference the extracted answer with any specific formatting or precision rules outlined in the prompt.
content: Always review the final answer to ensure it complies with all specified output constraints, such as required decimal places or data types. This step is crucial for preventing errors that arise from incorrect formatting, even if the core value is accurate.

title: Direct Information Retrieval
description: Prioritize direct extraction of information when keywords from the query appear explicitly in the text.
content: Scan financial notes for exact or close matches to keywords in the question. If a statement directly provides the answer, extract it without additional interpretation or calculation.

title: Contextual Note Filtering
description: Efficiently narrow down relevant sections by assessing note titles and initial content for alignment with the question's topic.
content: Before detailed reading, quickly review the titles or introductory sentences of financial notes. Focus only on sections clearly related to the question to improve search efficiency.

title: Answer Format Conformance
description: Always verify that the extracted numerical answer adheres to any specified output format requirements.
content: After identifying the correct value, ensure it matches requested precision, decimal places, or units, adjusting if necessary to meet strict output guidelines.

title: Prioritize Direct Information
